name: learning-biology-lab
description: Biology lab organism — cells, organelles, food webs, and classification networks on a biology-canvas.

name: learning-chemistry-lab
description: Chemistry lab organism — molecules, reactions, atoms, bonds, and arrows on a chemistry-canvas.

name: learning-math-lab
description: Math lab organism — plots functions, vectors, geometry, number lines, slope fields, and areas on a math-canvas.

name: learning-physics-lab
description: Physics lab organism — waves, fields, ray diagrams, pendulums, and circuits on a physics-canvas.

name: learning-probability-lab
description: Probability lab organism — dice, coins, random walks, and distributions on a math-canvas.

name: std-agent-activity-log
description: std-agent-activity-log as a Function

name: std-agent-assistant
description: Agent chat assistant: conversation, persistent memory, provider config (ChatGPT-style).

name: std-agent-builder
description: Agent app builder: prompt-driven plan/build/fix loop. Pick for NL app generators.

name: std-agent-chat-thread
description: std-agent-chat-thread as a Function

name: std-agent-classifier
description: std-agent-classifier as a Function

name: std-agent-completion
description: std-agent-completion as a Function

name: std-agent-context-window
description: std-agent-context-window as a Function

name: std-agent-conversation
description: std-agent-conversation as a Function

name: std-agent-fix-loop
description: std-agent-fix-loop as a Function

name: std-agent-learner
description: std-agent-learner as a Function

name: std-agent-memory
description: std-agent-memory as a Function

name: std-agent-pipeline
description: Agent pipeline: multi-step planned execution with session tree and progress logs.

name: std-agent-planner
description: std-agent-planner as a Function

name: std-agent-provider
description: std-agent-provider as a Function

name: std-agent-rag
description: std-agent-rag as a Function

name: std-agent-reviewer
description: Agent reviewer: code/doc review with RAG analysis and issue tracking.

name: std-agent-search
description: std-agent-search as a Function

name: std-agent-session
description: std-agent-session as a Function

name: std-agent-step-progress
description: std-agent-step-progress as a Function

name: std-agent-token-gauge
description: std-agent-token-gauge as a Function

name: std-agent-tool-call
description: std-agent-tool-call as a Function

name: std-agent-tool-loop
description: std-agent-tool-loop as a Function

name: std-agent-tutor
description: Agent tutor: personalized tutoring with quizzes, concepts, and student assessments.

name: std-anim-tick
description: std-anim-tick — sprite/effects animation clock atom. Advances every unit's frame (mod frameCount) and ages @entity.effects (decrement ttl, drop expired) every tick. Boards compose it via `uses` + a sibling trait-ref rebound (linkedEntity) to a board entity that carries `units` + `effects`, replacing the inlined animTick that was duplicated across 8+ game boards.

name: std-app-layout
description: std-app-layout — dashboard-layout chrome (sidebar nav + top bar with search + topBarActions + notifications) wrapping a single contentTrait slot. Use for list-style apps (CMS / helpdesk / CRM): the chrome stays stable while the consumer's content trait renders the data, and the consumer can add domain icons (cart / profile / help) via topBarActions. Bind once per orbital — the consumer rebinds linkedEntity via `XAppLayout = AppShell.traits.AppLayout -> XEntity` so multiple orbitals in one file each get their own chrome without colliding on a shared atom entity.

name: std-app-layout-bottomnav
description: Bottom-navigation chrome. Embeds std-app-layout with bottomnav layout pattern.

name: std-app-layout-minimal
description: Minimal chrome. Embeds std-app-layout with minimal layout pattern + a placeholder content trait so the main slot has visible content.

name: std-app-layout-topnav
description: Top-navigation chrome. Embeds std-app-layout with topnav layout pattern.

name: std-app-search
description: std-app-search — reusable global search: wraps a host content view and, on a SEARCH trigger, fetches the linked entity filtered by @config.searchField and renders the matching results, then CLEAR_SEARCH returns to the host view. Rebind to any entity; point @config.idleContent at the normal view and wire a trigger (e.g. AppLayout SEARCH) into SEARCH.

name: std-approval-gate
description: std-approval-gate — generic approval gate / approver workflow. Use when a user voices that an action must be reviewed, approved, signed off, or authorized by a privileged role before it proceeds (admin approval, supervisor sign-off, manager approval, gatekeeper review, editor approval, senior editor approval, reviewer approval, publishing approval, draft approval, content review, submit drafts then editors review and approve or reject, needs approval before going live, requires sign-off before publish). Persists ApprovalRequest rows + surfaces them in a review queue with Approve / Deny / Reject actions, optionally requiring a written rejection reason or feedback comment. On approval emits ApprovalGranted; on denial emits ApprovalDenied. Compose onto any orbital whose action you want to gate: the target orbital emits an external event, an ApprovalRequest row is created, and downstream effects wait on ApprovalGranted. Differs from std-mod-queue, which is content-moderation-specific.

name: std-asset-manifest
description: std-asset-manifest — logical-key→CDN-URL registry; compose into game orbitals to keep art consistent across scenes.

name: std-audit-capture
description: std-audit-capture — event-interceptor audit log / audit trail. Use when a user voices that a record's changes must be tracked, logged, or auditable (audit trail, track changes, change history, who edited what, mutation log, record changes, log every edit, edit history, modification log, who did what when, accountability log). Listens for the generic `EntityMutated` sentinel (rename via traitOverrides.events to the real mutation event, e.g. ContactSaved) and persists an AuditEntry row capturing actor, action, entity type/id, before/after snapshots, and timestamp. `captureEventTypes` filters on @payload.data.kind (empty = capture all). Compose with `std-lifecycle` to apply retention. Composable via `extraTraits` to interpose audit logging behind any mutating trait.

name: std-autocomplete
description: std-autocomplete — reusable inline typeahead: as the user types (AUTO_QUERY), fetches the linked entity filtered by @config.searchField and shows a compact suggestions list; picking one emits AUTO_SELECTED { id } for the consumer to open the record or fill a field. Rebind to any entity; configure searchField + the label field shown per suggestion.

name: std-billable-hour
description: std-billable-hour — generic billable-hour timesheet primitive for legal, agency, and consulting domains. Lists entries in `browsing`; opens entry detail in `viewing_single` with Invoice / Write Off actions.

name: std-board
description: std-board — generic N-column board primitive. `config.columns` is an array of column definitions ({ key, label, icon, variant }); the trait `array/map`s them on `BoardItemsLoaded` into `@entity.boards` where each board carries its filtered items + a count. The render is a `data-grid` over `@entity.boards` (one card per column) whose `renderItem` lambda nests a `data-list` over `@col.items` (the cards in that stage). N is config-driven — pass any number of columns; the outer `data-grid`'s `cols` is bound to `@config.gridCols` so the grid wraps if you exceed it. Topology = loading / viewing_board / viewing_card / adding / error — distinct from the cookie-cutter `loading/browsing/viewing_single/error` CRUD shape. App-level atoms (e.g. std-pipeline) compose std-board via `uses` and add a sibling persistor + extended source entity.

name: std-board-kanban-classic
description: Trello-style kanban — wide elevated columns side-by-side, sticky column headers with icon + label + count chip, dense cards with title, description, and a status badge. Same BoardItemBoard trait, same state machine, same emits/listens as std-board — only the bodyContent tree differs.

name: std-board-swim-lanes
description: Swim-lane board — full-width horizontal lanes per status, cards flow left-to-right within each lane. Lane header on top with icon + label + count; cards render in the lane body. Useful for HR-portal recruiting stages and project-manager team boards. Same BoardItemBoard trait, same state machine, same emits/listens as std-board — only the bodyContent tree differs.

name: std-board-timeline-roadmap
description: Timeline / roadmap board — horizontal time axis with quarters or months as columns; items render as cards inside their target column (item.stage = column.key). Mirrors the Linear / Asana / Productboard roadmap UX. Configure `columns` as ['Q1', 'Q2', 'Q3', 'Q4'] (or months) and the board lays them out left-to-right along a time axis. Same BoardItemBoard trait, same state machine, same emits/listens as std-board — only the bodyContent tree differs.

name: std-branching-logic
description: std-branching-logic — survey skip-logic rules. Lists rules in `browsing`; opens rule detail in `viewing_single` with Edit / Delete actions.

name: std-browse
description: std-browse as a Function — single primitive: data-grid. Owns the fetch + REFETCH (query/filter/page) state machine. Consumers configure fields/imageField/itemActions/cols/gap/variant; the atom renders the grid. Each `fields[]` entry accepts `{ name, label, variant }` plus optional `referenceEntity` + `displayField` to render a foreign-key column as the joined entity's display value (data-grid resolves @list[referenceEntity] at render time when both keys are present).

name: std-browse-command-palette
description: Command-palette browse — VS Code/Linear/Raycast floating overlay with search input header, tight result list, and kbd hint chips.

name: std-browse-dense
description: Spreadsheet-style dense browse — column-row data-table molecule with sticky header, monospaced numerics, status badges, tight row padding.

name: std-browse-feed
description: Activity feed — chronological card-per-item stream with avatar, title, meta, body, timestamp; hover-lift, gap-md rhythm.

name: std-browse-gallery
description: Gallery browse — image-first responsive card grid (Pinterest/Dribbble) using data-grid with auto-fit minCardWidth so it stays a grid from 3 cols above 1200px down to 1 col under 600px.

name: std-browse-map
description: Map browse — geo pin map: rows with lat/lng render as markers on a map-view, with a compact list beneath. For fleet vehicles, field technicians, store locations, delivery routes — anything with coordinates. Same BrowseItemBrowse trait + state machine as std-browse; only the bodyContent differs (a map instead of a grid).

name: std-cache-aside
description: std-cache-aside as a Function

name: std-calendar
description: std-calendar as a Function — owns the fetch + SELECT_DAY state machine; default bodyContent renders the month-grid + upcoming-events tree. Layer 3 variants override bodyContent to deliver agenda, week-timeline, etc. while inheriting the same trait, state machine, emits, and listens.

name: std-calendar-agenda-list
description: Day-grouped agenda — events organized by date with overline day headers (Today, Tomorrow, Mon Jun 5) and a time marker per event. Inherits std-calendar's fetch + SELECT_DAY state machine; only bodyContent differs from std-calendar's month-grid and std-calendar-week-timeline's 7-col timeline. For appointment queues, class-roster schedules, court-deadline lists where users scan a vertical chronological flow grouped by day.

name: std-calendar-week-timeline
description: Week-timeline calendar — events grouped by weekday in a 7-column timeline. The visible window normalizes to the payload's actual date range, so events stack into whichever day-of-week they fall on. Same CalendarEventCalendar trait, same state machine as std-calendar — only bodyContent differs. For weekly schedule views (fitness class week, healthcare appointment week).

name: std-cascade-on-delete
description: std-cascade-on-delete — relational `owns` cascade. Listens for a parent-deleted sentinel (`ParentDeleted` — rename via traitOverrides.events to the consumer's actual parent-deleted event), then deletes every dependent row whose `foreignKeyField` points at the deleted parent — via a self-driving loop (delete the head of the matching set, re-fetch until none remain). Emits `CascadeRowDeleted` per row and `CascadeCompleted` when done.

name: std-circuit-breaker
description: std-circuit-breaker as a Function

name: std-confirmation
description: std-confirmation as a Function

name: std-cooldown
description: std-cooldown — ability/action cooldown primitive; START begins the countdown, READY fires when it expires.

name: std-cross-reference
description: std-cross-reference — join-table primitive for N:M relationships. Listens for LinkRequested/UnlinkRequested/FETCH_LINKS_FOR and manages the junction collection (default CrossRefLink [persistent: cross_ref_links]). Rebind via linkedEntity to your consumer junction. Emits LinkCreated, LinkRemoved, LinksLoaded as external broadcasts for downstream consumers.

name: std-dashboard
description: std-dashboard — molecule composing std-stats + std-graphs + std-browse (stat cards top, charts middle, data list bottom). Browse is the data hub; Stats and Graphs listen for `BrowseItemLoaded -> ITEMS_LOADED`.

name: std-dashboard-grid-embedded
description: std-dashboard-grid-embedded — embeddable sibling of std-dashboard-grid-layout. Renders the same toolbar / filter / 12-tile grid contract WITHOUT wrapping it in dashboard-layout chrome (no sidebar, no appName, no nav). Use inside another app shell that already owns the surrounding chrome.

name: std-dashboard-grid-layout
description: std-dashboard-grid-layout — dashboard-layout chrome mirroring Looker Studio / Power BI: top-right toolbar slots (export / share / settings — feed `headerActions`), horizontal filter row (date range, search, dropdown filters), divider, then 12 numbered tile slots in a responsive simple-grid below. Slots default to EmptyTile so callers only bind what they need via traitOverrides.

name: std-dashboard-summary
description: Executive dashboard summary — 4 KPI tiles, status pill strip, and a primary line-chart panel. Standalone atom with its own loading/ready/error state machine; fetches a single SummaryRecord and renders inline.

name: std-data-erasure
description: std-data-erasure — GDPR Art. 17 right-to-be-forgotten queue. Tracks ErasureRequest rows through pending → grace → executed lifecycle with Cancel during grace. v1.0 ships the request lifecycle UI + knob set; auto-emit of ExecuteErasure after grace lands in v1.1 (loop-with-emit gap — docs/Almadar_Std_Factories.md). Target app/ atoms then own the actual delete or piiFields scrub via an ExecuteErasure listener.

name: std-date-range
description: std-date-range — compact calendar button (Looker / Tableau shape) that opens a popover containing the date-range-picker. Owns a DateRange runtime entity; DATE_RANGE_CHANGED persists @payload.from + @payload.to so cross-trait listeners can route the range into data atoms (Browse REFETCH_FILTER, Stats / Graphs requery).

name: std-detail-drawer
description: Drawer detail view. Embeds std-modal with drawer slot override.

name: std-drawer
description: std-drawer — drawer-slot atom. Renders ONLY to its `drawer` slot. The runtime's slot wrapper provides the backdrop + right-anchored panel + close-X chrome; the atom emits ONLY the panel body content. Host emits OPEN with payload {id, name, description} → renders body; CLOSE clears the slot. Stateless: state lives in the OPEN payload, not the entity, so the trait stays safe under consumer entity rebinds.

name: std-esign-flow
description: std-esign-flow — the send-for-signature flow: list signature requests, create one (pick document + recipient), resend or cancel, track status. Composes std-file-store + std-esign-request; no document-rendering or code-preview chrome.

name: std-esign-request
description: std-esign-request — specialized e-signature request tracker: send a document out for signing and track its status (draft/sent/viewed/signed/declined/expired). Just the request list + send/revoke; signature capture is std-signature-capture's job.

name: std-event-log
description: std-event-log — generic chronological event feed (Stripe activity log / GitHub PR timeline / Linear event feed style). Renders a vertical `timeline` with type-coded dots, a single horizontal chip-bar of kind filters (All + per-kind), reverse-chronological sort, and an inline form for logging events that weren't auto-captured. `config.kindOptions` maps each kind → status/icon/label so consuming molecules/organisms get type-coded visuals without re-implementing topology. Topology = loading / viewing / backfilling / error.

name: std-export
description: std-export — a row of export-format buttons. Predefined CSV/XLSX/PDF defaults; caller can override via config.formats. Emits EXPORT_REQUESTED { format, tileId } on the bus; host or sibling orbital generates the file.

name: std-file-store
description: std-file-store — the generic file/page tree primitive. A file-tree + config-driven data table over a hierarchical collection; opens a row in `viewing_single` with config-driven detail actions. Rebindable: pass `fields`/`itemActions`/`detailActions`/`tree` to retarget it at any hierarchical entity (files, wiki pages, note trees). Folds in the former std-page-tree via `reorderable` + a page field/action set.

name: std-filter
description: std-filter — single primitive: filter-group. Owns a FilterTarget runtime entity; every FILTER transition persists field/value so cross-trait listeners can route the latest selection into data atoms.

name: std-filtered-list
description: std-filtered-list — molecule composing slim Search/Filter/Browse/Pagination atoms

name: std-flag
description: std-flag — user content-report form. A std-form-advanced variation bound to a Flag entity (reason + details): the form persists the report and emits FlagSubmitted for cross-trait moderation listeners. Stays importable so a consumer orbital gains the Flag entity + report form via `uses` alongside its own primary entity.

name: std-form-advanced
description: std-form-advanced — full-typed form-section primitive. Hosts the `editing` state with a configurable `fields` array (each entry mirrors @almadar/ui's `form-section` field schema: `{ name, label, type?, values?, relation?, required? }`). Field-level constraints live in `config.fieldValidation` as `[{ field, format?: 'email'|'url'|'phone', regex?, minLength?, maxLength?, message? }]` — form-section surfaces inline errors when a submit violates a declared constraint. Agents drive shape by overriding `config.fields` + `config.fieldValidation` per-call-site.

name: std-gallery
description: std-gallery — image gallery primitive. Browses GalleryItems in a media-gallery grid; opens a lightbox modal for full-size viewing.

name: std-game-clock
description: std-game-clock — fixed-timestep authoritative clock games compose to drive time; emits GameClockTicked every 100ms while running.

name: std-generic-app
description: Generic fallback app: 8 reusable orbital factories (Contact, Item, Activity, Task, CalendarEvent, Widget, FeedPost, Note) under shared AppShell chrome. Picked by orbital-agent when no domain-specific organism is a confident match — agent maps prompt nouns to orbitals and supplies entity/title overrides via standard factory params.

name: std-geosearch
description: std-geosearch — location-aware search variant: search-input + map-view + range-slider for radius. Owns a GeoSearchResult runtime entity; GEO_SEARCH persists @payload.searchTerm/lat/lng/radiusKm so cross-trait listeners can route them into geo-aware data atoms.

name: std-graphs
description: std-graphs — embeddable chart atom. ITEMS_LOADED groups @payload.data by @config.categoryField (or @config.timeField when timeAxis=true) and aggregates per bucket. Multi-series rendering via @config.series (preformatted ChartSeries shape). Histograms via chartType=histogram pass pre-binned @payload.data through unchanged. Stacked / 100% stacked bars via @config.stack. Post-process sort + limit. Clicking a data point emits UI:@config.drillEvent with { label, value, seriesLabel? }. Drop into a dashboard alongside std-stats / std-browse.

name: std-graphs-area
description: Area chart view. Embeds std-graphs with area chart pattern override.

name: std-graphs-bar
description: Bar chart view. Embeds std-graphs with the bar chartType — vertical bars, one per category bucket. Default for distribution-of-categories visualisations.

name: std-graphs-donut
description: Donut chart view. Embeds std-graphs with the donut chartType — pie with a hollow centre that frees up space for a centered total/label. Reads better than pie when slices are similarly sized.

name: std-graphs-histogram
description: Histogram view. Embeds std-graphs with the histogram chartType — pre-binned bars passed through unchanged from @payload.data (the std-graphs atom skips its categorical bucketing for this type). Best for distributions of a continuous variable.

name: std-graphs-line
description: Line chart view. Embeds std-graphs with the line chartType — connected points over a continuous (typically time) axis. Best for trends and time-series.

name: std-graphs-pie
description: Pie chart view. Embeds std-graphs with the pie chartType — categorical share-of-total. Best when you have ≤6 categories that sum to a meaningful whole.

name: std-graphs-scatter
description: Scatter plot view. Embeds std-graphs with scatter plot pattern override.

name: std-hero-banner
description: Hero marquee — eyebrow + headline + lead + primary CTA, centered in an elevated card. Above-the-fold landing surface. Standalone atom; fetches one HeroPromo record and renders inline.

name: std-image-upload-multi
description: std-image-upload-multi — multi-image upload with drop zone, gallery preview, and per-batch progress.

name: std-lifecycle
description: std-lifecycle — age-based status lifecycle for a target entity. A periodic tick scans the target for records in `fromStatus` whose `dateField` is older than `ageDays`, and transitions each one to `toStatus` — every matching record, via a self-driving scan loop (process the head of a filtered fetch, persist the status, re-fetch until the set drains). Compose onto any orbital and bind `targetEntity` + the status/date knobs.

name: std-list
description: std-list as a Function

name: std-mod-queue
description: std-mod-queue — moderation queue for content review. Owns a ModQueueItem entity (one row per flagged target) and a single review trait that loads pending items, renders them in a data-grid with Approve / Reject / Escalate actions, and persists the reviewer's verdict back to the row.

name: std-modal
description: std-modal as a Function

name: std-multi-party-flow
description: std-multi-party-flow — Escrow.com / DocuSign / Stripe Connect style multi-party transaction. Header + summary (subject/amount/reference) + named parties (avatar/role/actor) + stage progress + your-turn panel + activity feed + dispute/cancel escape hatches. Topology = loading / awaiting / capturing_dispute / capturing_cancel / funded / released / disputed / cancelled / error.

name: std-notify-on-event
description: std-notify-on-event — cross-orbital wiring atom. Subscribes to events from another orbital (e.g. CheckoutOrbital emits OrderPlaced, OrderRecordOrbital reacts via this atom) and dispatches a notification. Use when one orbital needs to react to or be notified about events that happen in another orbital — the wiring goes through this atom's `EventOccurred` listener which the molecule renames via traitOverrides.events to the upstream event name. Resolves recipient from `recipients` (first entry; multi-recipient fan-out is a future extension) or falls back to `@user.id`. Skips dispatch when the recipient is in `suppressionList`. Emits in-app `notify` when `notifyChannel` is `"in-app"`, or persists a `NotificationRecord` row for out-of-band delivery when channel is `"email"` or `"sms"`.

name: std-pagination
description: std-pagination — single primitive: pagination control. PAGE accepts the Pagination component's `{page}` emit; ITEMS_LOADED accepts an upstream `{data:[ObjectSpec]}` payload and derives totalPages = ceil(len/pageSize). Molecules wire `BrowseItemLoaded -> ITEMS_LOADED` to keep it in lockstep with the data hub.

name: std-push
description: std-push as a Function

name: std-rate-limiter
description: std-rate-limiter as a Function

name: std-rating-review
description: std-rating-review — Airbnb / Amazon / Google Maps style review surface. Header with aggregate score + Write CTA, per-star distribution bars, working sort chips (recent / top rated / lowest), scrollable reviews list with author + stars + comment + helpful action. Topology = loading / viewing / composing / submitting / submitted / error.

name: std-recurrence
description: std-recurrence — recurring-rule definition + upcoming runs (Stripe Subscriptions / Cron Hub style). The viewing state shows a plain-English rule sentence and the next N dated runs as an agenda timeline. Per-run exception modal supports skip / reschedule. Topology = loading / defining_rule / viewing / awaiting_exception_decision / cancelled / error.

name: std-related
description: std-related as a Function

name: std-reminder-scheduler
description: std-reminder-scheduler — deadline reminders for a target entity. A periodic tick scans the target for records whose `dateField` deadline is within `offsetHours` of now and that have not been reminded yet, and emits `ReminderDue` for each — every matching record, via a self-driving scan loop. Marks each record `remindedAt` so it isn't reminded twice. Compose alongside std-notify-on-event to deliver the reminder.

name: std-rich-editor
description: std-rich-editor — wiki/CMS Notion-style block editor. Browses documents in `browsing`; opens the rich-block-editor on a selected row; persists block edits + publish status.

name: std-row-access-control
description: std-row-access-control — row-level access control gate / ownership filter / role-based row visibility. Use when a user voices that only certain users should see or edit certain records (only the owner can edit, private to owner, owner-only visibility, only see your own records, role-based visibility, admin-only access, visible to admins only, restricted to role, permission gate, ownership scoping, hide from other users, only show mine). Listens for `RowsLoaded` (rename via traitOverrides.events to the consumer's load event), filters `@payload.data` by `@user.id` (when `ownerField` is set) and/or `@user.role` (when `visibleRoles` is non-empty), re-emits `RowsFiltered` for downstream consumers (rename to wire into the UI atom's listen). Composable via `extraTraits` to interpose between a data fetch and a list/grid UI.

name: std-saved-search
description: std-saved-search — persisted query + alert subscription. Owns a SavedSearch entity (one row per saved query) and a single manage trait that lists saved searches in a data-grid, lets users toggle their alert subscription / rename the search / delete it, and persists each change.

name: std-scatter
description: std-scatter — embeddable scatter / bubble chart atom. ITEMS_LOADED maps @payload.data into {x,y,label,size,color} points using configured fields (no aggregation step — distinct from std-graphs). Drop into a dashboard alongside std-stats / std-graphs for two-numeric correlation views.

name: std-search
description: std-search — single primitive: search-input. Owns a SearchResult runtime entity; SEARCH persists @payload.searchTerm (matching SearchInput's emit shape) so cross-trait listeners can route it into data atoms (Browse REFETCH_QUERY).

name: std-selection
description: std-selection as a Function

name: std-sidebar-meta
description: Right-rail meta panel — Linear/Jira/GitHub-style issue sidebar. Stacked label/value rows grouped with dividers. Standalone atom; fetches one SidebarRecord and renders inline.

name: std-signature-capture
description: std-signature-capture — a drawn-signature pad. The signer draws, clears, or submits; on submit the signature persists and the atom emits SignatureSubmitted. One job: capture a signature and report when done.

name: std-stats
description: std-stats — embeddable analytics-card atom. ITEMS_LOADED aggregates @payload.data per @config.metrics (count|sum|avg|min|max|variance with optional filter, baselineFilter, format, icon, max, target, trend, trendPolarity, sparkline) and renders one stat-display per entry. Variance metrics compute actual - baseline using `filter` + `baselineFilter` lambdas and report the signed delta as trend. clickEvent emits UI:{clickEvent} { metricLabel } when a card is clicked. Drop into a dashboard alongside std-browse / std-search / std-filter.

name: std-stats-hero-metric
description: Hero metric — one elevated card, one oversize number. The headline KPI for executive landing pages and finance hero dashboards.

name: std-stats-kpi-tiles
description: KPI tiles — Stripe-dashboard style. 3–4 large numeric tiles arranged in a horizontal grid: huge typography number, label below, optional change indicator and sparkline hint. For trading dashboards, finance trackers, ecommerce metrics, payment volume. Same StatsItemStats trait, same aggregation pipeline, same listens as std-stats — only the bodyContent tree differs.

name: std-stats-sparkline-row
description: Sparkline tile grid — chart-first card tiles where the mini trend line is the visual centerpiece, with label above and value+delta footer. For trend-led overviews: telemetry, weekly active users, traffic shape, observability at a glance.

name: std-status-lifecycle
description: Infra substrate: data-driven entity status lifecycle. The statuses and the allowed transitions are config data over a fixed single-state topology, so a host organism can expand or collapse its lifecycle without forking. Compose onto an entity, rename the ChangeStatusRequested listen to the host's status-change action, and supply `states` + `transitions`. A requested move is validated against the `transitions` table (illegal moves are rejected), then the new value is written to `statusField` on the target entity and StatusChanged is emitted. Surfaces nothing — the host renders the badge and buttons.

name: std-step-flow
description: std-step-flow — generic N-step sequential decision chain (Stripe Connect / TurboTax / Vercel deploy style). `config.steps` is an array of step definitions `{ key, label, description, icon, allowedRoles?: [string] }`; the trait projects the current step into a prominent body card and a `wizard-progress` stepper bar at the top. When a step declares `allowedRoles`, only users whose `@user.role` is in that list can advance through it. One primary button ADVANCEs along the chain; at the last step the same button finalizes (label/icon/variant swap via entity binding, state routed via guard). REJECT/ESCALATE terminate. Topology = loading / running / approved / rejected / escalated / error.

name: std-step-flow-cards-deck
description: Full-page card deck step flow — each step is rendered as one large centered card occupying the viewport with the step icon, title, and description prominent; progress-dots at the bottom indicate position in the sequence; primary advance + back controls flank the dots. Same StepFlowReview trait, same state machine, same emits/listens as std-step-flow — only the bodyContent tree differs. Suited for mobile-flavored onboarding, marketing-campaign creation flows, and any single-focus sequential UX.

name: std-step-flow-progress-vertical
description: Header progress bar above a vertical step list — full-width progress affordance up top, every step as a row card below.

name: std-tabs
description: std-tabs — embeddable tab-navigation atom. Renders a tabs strip from @config.tabs ([{id, label, icon?, badge?, content?}]); clicking a tab updates @entity.activeTab so only the matching @trait.X content shows. Events remap at the call site for routing, persistence, analytics.

name: std-tabs-layout
description: std-tabs-layout — chrome with std-tabs strip; consumer overrides config.tabs[].content with @trait.X references.

name: std-tabs-segmented-pills
description: Segmented-pills tabs — iOS-style segmented control: pill-shaped tabs sit in a single rounded background bar, no underline, active tab carries a filled background. Compact and modern; ideal for settings UIs and mode-switchers. Same TabsItemTabs trait, state machine, emits/listens as std-tabs — only the bodyContent tree differs.

name: std-tabs-sidebar-rail
description: Sidebar-rail tabs — vertical nav rail on the left listing tab items as a Stack with the active one highlighted; the matching @trait.X content trait renders to the right. Ideal for app-shell side nav, settings pages with many sections, and profile pages. Same TabsItemTabs trait, state machine, emits/listens as std-tabs — only the bodyContent tree differs.

name: std-tag-taxonomy
description: std-tag-taxonomy — hierarchical category tag tree (parent/child). TagBrowse fetches Tag rows on INIT, renders the active level via data-grid, and on SELECT_TAG drills into a tag's children by binding @entity.id to the chosen parent.

name: std-thread
description: std-thread — forum-style nested replies with collapse + flat-mode toggle. Owns ThreadPost (persistent). flat:true covers 1:1 linear inquiry threads; flat:false renders forum-style nested. INIT fetches; REPLY persists a child post under @payload.parentId. Layer 3 variants override `bodyContent` to deliver chat-bubble, email-threaded, or linear-comments UX while inheriting the same trait, state machine, emits, and listens.

name: std-thread-chat-bubbles
description: Chat-bubbles thread — iMessage/WhatsApp DM style: two-party back-and-forth with sender-vs-receiver bubble alignment and tint.

name: std-thread-comments-linear
description: Nested threaded comments — Disqus/Hacker News style: indented reply tree, inline reply composer on every comment card, and a visible replies group per parent. For forum discussions, blog comments, document review threads, project-manager issue comments.

name: std-thread-email-threaded
description: Email-threaded — Gmail/Outlook/Mail.app split-pane: left message list (sender, subject, preview), right pane shows the focused message with from/to/subject header and quoted-reply nested tree. For helpdesk email threads, support tickets, document discussions that read like email.

name: std-version-history
description: std-version-history — wiki/CMS revision history with diff + rollback. Browses revisions; opens detail in `viewing_single`; compares two revisions in `comparing` with intrinsic version-diff; persists rollback.

name: std-vote
description: std-vote — forum-style up/down vote tally with single-vote-per-user constraint. Owns a Vote entity; VOTE persists the new direction (toggling to none when re-clicking the active arrow) and emits VoteCasted so cross-trait listeners can recompute aggregates.

name: std-wizard
description: std-wizard — generic N-step data-collection wizard (Vercel deploy / GitHub repo create / Stripe Connect onboarding style). Each step has its own form fields. Topology = loading / running / completed / cancelled / error. ADVANCE is guard-routed: not-last-step advances; last-step transitions to completed. Distinct from std-step-flow (approval-decision) — wizard collects data via form-section per step, step-flow collects yes/no decisions.

name: std-wizard-form
description: std-wizard-form — canonical molecule-level wizard example (3-step sign-up flow). Wizards own their state machine at molecule tier.
